title: Direct CUHK doctoral record or departmental profile
claim: Tighten the Ph.D. chapter beyond profile-level biography by finding a university-hosted record that directly names Sean Xiang and the degree context.
desiredEvidence: University profile, thesis repository page, graduation record, or lab / departmental page
whyItMatters: This would upgrade the graduate-era chapter from a connective public profile into a firmer institutional record, letting the opening chronology read less cautiously.
targetEffect: Move the chapter from third-party connective sourcing toward direct academic confirmation.
notes: Do not overstate if the only public trail remains secondary. The right win is a university-hosted page, not a scraped people directory or AI summary.

title: Beckman / UC Irvine scientist-era institutional page
claim: Replace the current bridge-chapter dependence on one expansion profile with a direct institutional mention tying Sean Xiang to the Beckman Laser Institute at UC Irvine.
desiredEvidence: Institute staff page, archived lab profile, event bio, research page, or university-hosted publication / author profile
whyItMatters: The California transition is narratively important because it explains the move from research to company-building. A direct institutional record would make that bridge materially stronger.
targetEffect: Upgrade the Irvine chapter from plausible connective narrative into a cleaner, source-backed transition.
notes: Best target is an institutional page with role wording, not a generic citation index unless it clearly ties the affiliation to the period.

title: Direct proof for the USTC-at-16 acceleration claim
claim: The origin chapter currently preserves the pace of the story without asserting the USTC-at-16 detail as fact. A direct source is needed before the site becomes more explicit.
desiredEvidence: University-hosted bio, interview, alumni profile, conference biography, or contemporaneous publication bio with the acceleration detail
whyItMatters: This is the single highest-leverage evidence upgrade for the opening chapter. Without it, the premium editorial treatment should stay intentionally restrained.
targetEffect: Unlock a firmer first chapter and reduce dependence on internal synthesis for the site’s opening claim.
notes: If no direct public source appears, keep the current language restrained rather than stretching weaker evidence.

title: Earlier founding-year company document or founder bio
claim: The site now has a strong 2013 release trail, but it avoids pretending to have exact founding-day paperwork. An earlier public company document could narrow that gap.
desiredEvidence: Archived company bio, early pitch / event page, incorporation-era profile, or dated company document naming Sean Xiang as founder or CEO
whyItMatters: This would tighten the handoff from research formation into the founder chapter and make the founding section feel even more archival.
targetEffect: Shorten the leap between pre-founder context and the first public 2013 release trail.
notes: The current chapter is strong. This is a refinement pass, not a blocker.

title: Conference visuals and article snippets only where source quality is strong
claim: The AI-era chapter is the best candidate for richer media treatment because it has direct announcement-style evidence and a live public release surface.
desiredEvidence: Conference booth visuals, speaker graphics, article snippets, or screenshots tied to direct public URLs
whyItMatters: This would deepen the artifact system without introducing toy placeholders. The media should behave like evidence, not decoration.
targetEffect: Turn the AI-era chapter into a more museum-like closing section with real, source-backed visual texture.
notes: Only ship media when it can be tied to a real public page or preserved capture. Avoid speculative visuals.
